mnemonics: BOOTSTRAPS PULL UP DATA: Bootstrapping pulls new datasets (with replacement) from the original data to estimate sampling variability.
keyPoints: Bootstrap is a resampling technique used to estimate the sampling distribution of a statistic.
It involves drawing many samples (with replacement) from the original data, each the same size as the original dataset.
It is particularly useful for estimating standard errors and confidence intervals when theoretical formulas are complex or unavailable.
commonMistakes: Assuming bootstrapped samples are drawn without replacement (they must be drawn WITH replacement).
Using bootstrapping on datasets that are too small or have heavy dependencies, like time series data, without appropriate modifications.
